data.title: Unitree Robotics' IPO application on the Science and Technology Innovation Board has been accepted by the Shanghai Stock Exchange, with a planned fundraising of 4.202 billion yuan.
data.description: The Shanghai Stock Exchange has accepted the IPO application of Unitree Automation Co., Ltd. for listing on the Science and Technology Innovation Board. The company plans to raise 4.202 billion yuan. (Jinshi)

data.title: OKX launches exclusive VIP World Cup merchandise activities, supporting customized jerseys.
data.description: According to official news, OKX has launched a VIP exclusive World Cup-themed gift box, including jerseys, soccer balls, beach towels, and wristbands, among other products. The jerseys can be customized with the user's English name and number. Users who become or maintain their OKX VIP status before March 25th can claim the gift box. How to claim: OKX APP – Homepage Banner. In addition, OKX will also conduct interactive lucky draws on social platforms such as X and OKX Planet. Users who participate in the activities and submit their customized English name and number will have a chance to win the World Cup gift box.

data.title: Hyperliquid Sees $1.5 Billion Surge in Oil-Linked Trading Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Hyperliquid experienced a significant increase in trading volume, reaching $1.5 billion in oil-linked trades within a 24-hour period. According to NS3.AI, this surge is attributed to traders shifting their focus to energy-related bets amid the ongoing conflict in Iran. The heightened trading activity has been linked to renewed bullish sentiment on HYPE, as investors respond to geopolitical tensions affecting the energy markets.

data.title: Sky Co-Founder Rune Closes ETH Position with Loss, Shifts Focus to Brent Crude Oil
data.description: Sky co-founder Rune has closed his long position in Ethereum, incurring a loss of approximately $257,000. According to NS3.AI, Rune has shifted his investment strategy by increasing a 7x leveraged long position in Brent crude oil. Additionally, he has placed TWAP orders to continuously adjust other macro positions.

data.title: HSBC, JPMorgan, and Natixis Join Foreign Lenders in Three-Year Facility
data.description: HSBC, JPMorgan, and Natixis are among the twelve foreign banks involved in a three-year financial arrangement. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the participation of these major financial institutions in the facility. The initiative aims to enhance cross-border financial cooperation and strengthen international banking relationships. This move is seen as a strategic effort to bolster global financial networks and facilitate smoother transactions across borders. The involvement of these prominent banks underscores the significance of the facility in promoting international financial collaboration.

data.title: Italy's Energy Minister Agrees with EU: Russian Gas Should Not Return to Europe
data.description: Italy's Energy Minister has expressed agreement with the European Union's stance that Russian natural gas should not be allowed back into Europe. According to Jin10, the minister emphasized the importance of diversifying energy sources and reducing dependency on Russian gas. This aligns with the EU's broader strategy to enhance energy security and transition towards more sustainable energy solutions. The minister's statement reflects ongoing geopolitical tensions and the EU's efforts to mitigate risks associated with energy reliance on Russia.

data.title: Italy Engages in Talks to Address Qatar Gas Supply Disruption
data.description: Italy's Energy Minister has announced ongoing discussions with several countries, including the United States, Azerbaijan, and Algeria, to mitigate the impact of disrupted natural gas supplies from Qatar. According to Jin10, these negotiations aim to secure alternative sources and ensure energy stability for Italy. The minister emphasized the importance of diversifying energy partnerships to safeguard against future supply chain interruptions. This strategic move comes amid growing concerns over energy security and the need for reliable international cooperation in the energy sector.

data.title: Italy's Trade Balance with EU Improves in January
data.description: Italy's trade balance with the European Union showed improvement in January, recording a deficit of 1.138 billion euros, compared to the previous deficit of 2.447 billion euros. According to Jin10, this marks a significant reduction in the trade deficit, indicating a positive shift in Italy's trade relations with EU countries. The data suggests a potential strengthening of Italy's export sector or a reduction in imports, contributing to the narrowed trade gap. This development could have implications for Italy's economic outlook and its trade policies within the EU.

data.title: Exploring the Links Between Security Lines and Social Safety Nets
data.description:
A recent episode of Everybody’s Business delves into the intricate connections between lengthy security lines, the Epstein files, and the deteriorating social safety net in the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how these seemingly unrelated issues are intertwined, impacting the daily lives of Americans.

The episode examines the inefficiencies in airport security processes, which often result in extended wait times for travelers. These delays are not just a matter of inconvenience but are symptomatic of broader systemic issues within the country's infrastructure.

Furthermore, the discussion extends to the Epstein files, shedding light on how high-profile cases can influence public perception and trust in governmental and social institutions. The episode suggests that these cases reveal deeper flaws in the justice system and its ability to hold powerful individuals accountable.

Lastly, the episode addresses the weakening social safety net, emphasizing how economic disparities and policy decisions have left many Americans vulnerable. The conversation underscores the need for comprehensive reforms to address these interconnected challenges effectively.

Through this exploration, Everybody’s Business aims to provide a nuanced understanding of how these issues are interlinked, urging policymakers and the public to consider the broader implications of these systemic problems.

data.title: Renowned Architect Bjarke Ingels Faces Macroeconomic Challenges
data.description: Danish designer Bjarke Ingels, recognized globally for his architectural prowess, is experiencing the impact of macroeconomic shifts. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how even prominent figures in architecture are not exempt from broader economic influences. Ingels, known for his innovative designs, is navigating these challenges as they affect the industry and his projects. Despite his reputation and success, the current economic climate presents obstacles that require strategic adaptation. Ingels' situation underscores the pervasive nature of economic fluctuations and their ability to affect diverse sectors, including architecture.

data.title: Bangladesh Plans Stable Power Supply Amid Summer Demand
data.description: Bangladesh is taking measures to maintain a steady power supply throughout the summer months without increasing domestic electricity prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's commitment to meeting the rising energy demands during the hotter season. Authorities are focused on balancing the need for consistent power with the economic impact on consumers, ensuring that households and businesses can rely on uninterrupted electricity without facing higher costs. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to manage energy resources efficiently while supporting economic stability.

data.title: Coinbase launches US stock perpetual contracts, open to eligible users outside the US.
data.description: Coinbase announced the launch of perpetual contracts for US stocks, open to eligible users outside the US, supporting 24/7 leveraged trading of US-listed stocks. The product offers contract trading for multiple US stocks, including Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Tesla, as well as ETFs such as SPY and QQQ. Leverage is supported up to 10x for individual stocks and up to 20x for ETFs, settled in USDC. Coinbase stated that this product aims to meet the global market's demand for 24/7 US stock trading and plans to expand to more stocks, indices, and other asset classes in the future.

data.title: IEA Urges Remote Work and Reduced Travel to Address Rising Oil Prices
data.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has recommended measures such as working from home, carpooling, and reducing air travel to mitigate the impact of escalating oil prices. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that these suggestions come amid concerns over the global energy market's volatility. The IEA emphasizes that these actions could help decrease oil demand and stabilize prices, which have been affected by geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions. The agency's report highlights the importance of collective efforts in energy conservation to address the current challenges faced by the oil industry. It also suggests that governments and businesses should encourage flexible work arrangements and promote public transportation to reduce reliance on oil. The IEA's call to action aims to foster sustainable practices and enhance energy security globally.

data.title: Ripple Survey Reveals Finance Leaders' Views on Stablecoins and Digital Assets
data.description: Ripple conducted a survey involving over 1,000 global finance leaders, revealing significant insights into the perceived benefits of stablecoins and digital assets. According to NS3.AI, 74% of respondents believe that stablecoins have the potential to enhance cash-flow efficiency and unlock working capital. Additionally, 70% of the surveyed finance leaders expressed the need for a digital asset solution to maintain competitiveness in the evolving financial landscape.

data.title: Retailer Reports Decline in Pretax Profit Despite Sales Growth
data.description: A major retailer has reported a 32% drop in pretax profit, amounting to £22.4 million, despite experiencing a 4.8% increase in like-for-like sales during the first half of its financial year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial challenges faced by the company amid rising operational costs and market pressures. The retailer's performance reflects broader industry trends, where sales growth does not necessarily translate into higher profitability due to various economic factors. The company is expected to implement strategic measures to address these challenges and improve its financial outlook.

data.title: European Central Bank Reaffirms Commitment to 2% Inflation Target
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has reiterated its dedication to achieving a medium-term inflation target of 2%. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting comments from Governing Council member Francois Villeroy de Galhau, who emphasized the importance of stabilizing inflation at this level. The ECB's commitment comes amid ongoing economic challenges and fluctuating market conditions. Villeroy de Galhau's statement underscores the central bank's focus on maintaining price stability as a key priority. The ECB continues to monitor economic indicators closely to ensure that inflation aligns with its target, aiming to foster a stable economic environment.

data.title: AI TRENDS | X to Release Major AI Algorithm Update Next Week
data.description: X is set to release a significant update to its artificial intelligence algorithm next week, according to NS3.AI. Elon Musk announced that the update will be open-sourced, allowing broader access and collaboration. This move is part of X's ongoing efforts to enhance transparency and innovation in AI development.

data.title: Vincorion Shares Surge 14% Above Offer Price on Opening
data.description: Shares in Vincorion began trading at €19.30, marking a 14% increase over the initial offer price of €17 each. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strong market debut for the company. The opening price reflects investor confidence and interest in Vincorion's market potential. This development comes amid a broader context of fluctuating stock markets and economic uncertainties. Vincorion's performance will be closely watched by analysts and investors as it navigates the current financial landscape.

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer Volume Declines as Miner Revenues Drop
data.description: Bitcoin's long-term holder transfer volume experienced a month-over-month decline across all age cohorts, according to NS3.AI. VanEck's mid-March 2026 report highlighted a 31% decrease in overall onchain transfer volume. Despite a steady miner selling pressure, miner revenues fell by 11% month over month, while miner outflows to exchanges increased by just 1% in BTC terms.
